memory:memory/2026-04-10.md:263:296.snippet: - Universal bytecode translator architecture - Philosophical notes on self-hosting 3. **flux-isa-v2-proposal.md** (7.5K) — Unified ISA design: - Fixed 4-byte instructions (like Lua) - All arithmetic 3-operand (unified, no Format C/E split) - Flag-based conditional jumps (CMP+JEQ/JNE/JLT/JGT) - Memory operations (LOAD/STORE/LOADB/STOREB) - Function calls (CALL/RET with link register) - SYSCALL for platform I/O - A2A as opcodes (TELL/ASK/DELEGATE/BROADCAST) - Speculative execution (CLONE/ROLLBACK/PEEK) ### Key Insights from Research - **Lua's fixed-width instructions** are 20-30% faster to dispatch — FLUX v2 should adopt this - **Erlang's actor model** maps perf
